verdict_reasoning: LYN is a non-receptor Src-family tyrosine kinase anchored to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-myristoylation and palmitoylation. While LYN does localize to the plasma membrane and associates with cell-surface receptors (BCR, CD40, CD19) for signaling, these are intracellular protein-protein interactions. LYN has no transmembrane domain, no extracellular domain, and no documented mechanism for reaching the outer (extracellular) face of the plasma membrane. No cell-state-induced surface display, no stable post-translational TM-partner tethering, no lysosomal exocytosis delivery, no tissue-restricted surface localization, and no documented cancer-associated ecto-presentation have been reported for this kinase.
